title: Versioning, Delete Markers, Object Lock, and Restore · Mastery Check
summary: A production board compares near-miss TypeScript and AWS decisions for versioning, delete markers, object lock, and restore and accepts only runtime validation, bounded ownership, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit controlled AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for immutable-retention and recovery runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, list versions and delete markers, resolve current reads, restore by removing a marker or copying a version, and preserve audit identity.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, apply governance, compliance, legal hold, retention date, and authorized bypass by version with irreversible-risk review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Versioning State Transition
steps.instruction:
Order the Versioning State Transition S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript storage-delivery evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Versioning State Transition.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Versioning State Transition assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Versioning State Transition, keep Versioning State Transition connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 7090 during a browser upload compatibility rollout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to narrow versioning state and require version-specific retention and restore evidence; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Versioning State Transition has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Versioning State Transition, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Suspending versioning returns a bucket to its original unversioned behavior. At S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Restore Drill Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict runtime validation, authorization, custody, integrity, range, stream, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 6376 during a browser upload compatibility rollout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to narrow versioning state and require version-specific retention and restore evidence; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, run sampled restore drills with authorization, known bytes, metadata, dependencies, time objectives, audit evidence, and safe cleanup.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Restore Drill Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Restore Drill Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 7995 during a regional recovery and budget exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to narrow versioning state and require version-specific retention and restore evidence; keep AbortSignal and stream cleanup under one named owner; then change object version, signed header, or cache dimension and reject stale authority. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
